appraisal_events.evidence: Il me fit comprendre ... l'illusion dont Robert de Saint-Loup était victime à l'égard de Rachel ... À une distance convenable ... un nez si fin, si pur ... Il l'aimait déjà.
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator reveals that Robert's love is born from an optical and dream-like illusion created by distance and fantasy, showing Robert governed by an unreal image rather than by clear perception.

appraisal_events.evidence: Cette fiction ... de ne pas me reconnaître ... son corps avait été admirablement dressé ... à un certain nombre de dissimulations de bienséance, et, comme un parfait comédien, il pouvait ... jouer ... des rôles différents.
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator emphasizes Robert's mastery in controlling his expressions and playing the appropriate social role, which elevates him locally as a model of self-discipline and social skill.

status_effects.explanation: He is presented as a dupe of an emotional illusion, which diminishes his lucidity and judgment in this episode.
His ability to instantly assume a social role and conceal his reactions gives him local performative authority.
